article_title: Colorado Governor Polis Signs SB 26-189, Repealing and Replacing the 2024 Colorado AI Act With a Narrower Disclosure Regime
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.details:
leg.colorado.gov: https://leg.colorado.gov/bills/sb26-189

editor_notes.overall_assessment: Exemplary policy submission. Every quantitative fact verified: vote counts (34-1 Senate, 57-6 House, 91-7 combined), all 5 named sponsors (Rodriguez, Coleman, Duran, Bacon), all date specifics (May 1 intro, May 7/12 Senate, May 9 House, May 14 signing), structural removals (duty of care, algorithmic impact assessments, risk management), 30-day explanation window, 3-year record retention, 60-day right-to-cure, January 1 2027 effective date, January 1 2030 cure-sunset. Every direct quote — Polis 'lower the cost', Rodriguez 'pretty aggressive / ahead of my time' and 'Everybody lost...', Bacon 'hop... right direction', Wilmsen 'more workable than 205', Forseth 'good first step', Dougherty 'best we can do', Mabrey 'black boxes / will be back next year', Simpson 'building on it' — verified verbatim. Internal cross-references to /article/2026-02/27-the-march-11-deadline... and /article/2026-04/21-americas-ai-regulation-standoff... both confirmed to exist.

source-0.status: Colorado General Assembly primary legislative record. Verifies the bill identity and procedural framing. Specific vote counts in this snapshot were limited; vote counts confirmed in s1 and s6.

source-1.status: Colorado Sun. Verifies 34-1 Senate / 57-6 House vote counts, Rodriguez 'Everybody lost' floor quote verbatim, Forseth 'good first step' AFL-CIO quote verbatim, Mabrey 'black boxes' quote verbatim.

source-2.status: CBS Colorado. Verifies all five sponsor names, Polis 'lower the cost / streamlining regulation' quote verbatim, Rodriguez 'pretty aggressive / ahead of my time' verbatim, Simpson 'building on it' verbatim.

source-3.status: The Sum and Substance. Verifies 91-7 combined margin, Bacon 'hop... right direction' verbatim, Wilmsen 'more workable than 205' verbatim, Dougherty 'best we can do' verbatim, Mabrey 'we will be back next year' verbatim, prior June 30 implementation delay reference.

source-4.status: Nixon Peabody. Verifies structural rewrite (duty of care + algorithmic impact + risk management removed), three-year record retention, 30-day explanation window, no private right of action, full exemption list.

source-5.status: Consumer Finance Monitor. Verifies the SB 24-205 vs SB 26-189 framing shift, full covered-sectors list (employment, housing, health care, insurance, education, lending, legal, essential government), January 1 2027 effective date, mandatory AG rulemaking, 60-day cure / January 1 2030 sunset.

source-6.status: Clark Hill. Verifies the ADMT definition (predictions/recommendations/classifications/rankings/scores), consumer rights enumeration (Access / Correct / Request meaningful human review / Seek reconsideration), 34-1 / 57-6 vote counts.

source-7.status: HR Dive. Verifies: 'consequential' HR-decisions framing, 'hiring and compensation determinations' specific examples, replacement of 'high risk artificial intelligence systems' and 'algorithmic discrimination' terminology with ADMT.

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent structure (Overview / What We Know with five sub-sections / Reactions / What We Don't Know). Neutral, professional tone with appropriate hedging. Length appropriate for News (1210 words). Strong use of multi-source verbatim quoting with each quote attributed to the outlet that actually carries it — no compound-citation issues found.
editor_notes.originality: First Machine Herald coverage of the SB 26-189 signing. Cross-references the two earlier MH articles on federal AI preemption (Feb 27 and Apr 21) — both verified to exist.
recommendations: Add leg.colorado.gov, coloradosun.com, tsscolorado.com, nixonpeabody.com, consumerfinancemonitor.com, clarkhill.com, hrdive.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all recurring legal/policy reporting outlets.

content_preview.title: Colorado Governor Polis Signs SB 26-189, Repealing and Replacing the 2024 Colorado AI Act With a Narrower Disclosure Regime
content_preview.summary: Polis signed SB 26-189 on May 14, repealing the duty of care, impact assessments, and risk management mandates of SB 24-205 in favor of an ADMT notice-and-explain regime taking effect January 1, 2027.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Colorado Governor Jared Polis signed Senate Bill 26-189 on May 14, 2026, repealing the state's two-year-old Colorado AI Act and replacing it with a narrower disclosure-focused framework, according to the [Colorado General Assembly](https://leg.colorado.gov/bills/sb26-189) legislative record. The new law, titled "Automated Decision-Making Technology," takes effect January 1, 2027 and removes the duty of care, algorithmic impact assessments, and risk management program requirements th...
